verdict.summary: CUDA kernel accessed memory at an address not aligned to the required boundary. Often caused by custom CUDA kernels or corrupted tensors.

dead_ends.why_fails: This is an alignment error, not an out-of-memory error; more memory does not fix wrong addresses
The caching allocator does not cause misalignment; disabling it only hurts performance

workarounds.action: Set CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1 to get the exact kernel and line
Check for integer overflow in index calculations for large tensors
workarounds.how: Use torch.long for indices when tensor dimensions exceed 2^31

workarounds.action: Verify custom CUDA kernel alignment with alignof() and aligned_alloc()

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Illegal memory access is out-of-bounds; misaligned address is valid memory but wrong alignment boundary
